title: Avoid Using Identical Expressions on Both Sides of a Binary Operator
description: Using the same value on both sides of a binary operator can lead to bugs or unnecessary code complexity. This is particularly true for logical operators, where it could be a result of a copy/paste error, or simply duplicated code that needs to be simplified. Similarly, for bitwise operators and most binary mathematical operators, using the same value on both sides of an operator can yield predictable results, indicating that the code can be simplified.
recommendation: Check your code logic and ensure you are not using identical expressions on both sides of a binary operator. Simplify your code where necessary.
impact: This practice could lead to code defects, unnecessary code complexity and duplication, or predictable results that should be simplified.

checks.message: Check your code logic and ensure you are not using identical expressions on both sides of a binary operator.
